3.happening: Create the empty result — it will hold one row per level.
3.why: Level-order output groups values by depth.
3.invariant: result[i] will be the values at depth i.

4.happening: Count the array slots describing the tree.
4.why: We need to know how many potential nodes there are.
4.invariant: n is the length of the description array.

5.happening: The description is empty — there is no tree.
5.why: An empty array means no nodes at all.
5.invariant: An empty tree has an empty traversal.

5.happening: The tree has at least one node — we'll build and sweep it.
5.why: A non-empty array describes a real tree.

6.invariant: The answer for an empty tree is [].

7.happening: Start an empty list of real tree nodes.
7.why: We build node objects the visualization can draw.
7.invariant: nodes holds every non-null value as a node.

8.happening: Start a lookup from node id to node object.
8.why: BFS needs O(1) access to a node by its id.
8.invariant: by_id[i] is the node stored at slot i.

9.happening: Start a status list for every slot.
9.why: It records each node's state: unseen, queued, or visited.

10.happening: Loop over every slot to initialize its status.
10.invariant: Slots below i already have a status.

12.happening: Start the child-slot walker just past the root.
12.why: Children are handed out in array order starting at slot 1.
12.invariant: child is the next unassigned array slot.

13.happening: Loop over every slot to build the nodes.
13.why: Each real value becomes a node linked to its children.
13.invariant: Slots below i are already built.

14.happening: Only real values (not null) become nodes.
14.why: null slots are placeholders for missing children.
14.invariant: A null slot is skipped entirely.

15.happening: Create node {i} with its value and empty child links.
15.why: We fill in the children from the following slots.
15.invariant: A fresh node starts with no children.

16.happening: Is there a slot left to be this node's left child?
16.why: Children come from the next array slots in order.
16.invariant: child points at the candidate left child.

17.happening: Is that slot a real node (not null)?
17.why: Only non-null slots become actual children.
17.invariant: A null candidate leaves the left child empty.

18.happening: Link slot {child} as this node's left child.
18.why: It's the next real node in level order.
18.invariant: left now points at a real child id.

19.happening: Move past this slot whether or not it was a child.
19.why: Each slot is claimed exactly once.

20.happening: Is there a slot left to be this node's right child?
20.why: The right child is the slot right after the left one.
20.invariant: child points at the candidate right child.

21.happening: Is that slot a real node (not null)?
21.why: Only non-null slots become actual children.
21.invariant: A null candidate leaves the right child empty.

22.happening: Link slot {child} as this node's right child.
22.why: It's the next real node in level order.
22.invariant: right now points at a real child id.

23.why: Each slot is claimed exactly once.

24.happening: Add the finished node to the tree.
24.why: It now has its value and child links.
24.invariant: nodes grows by one built node.

25.happening: Record the node under its id for fast lookup.
25.why: BFS will fetch nodes by id.
25.invariant: by_id maps every id to its node.

26.invariant: Nodes are processed front to back.

27.why: Level order starts at the root.
27.invariant: The root is the first node processed.

28.invariant: A node enters the queue at most once.

29.happening: Set the queue's front marker to 0.
29.why: It points at the next node to visit.
29.invariant: Nodes before head are already visited.

30.happening: Reset the current-node marker to -1 (none yet).
30.why: No node is being visited before the sweep starts.

31.happening: The queue holds nodes — process the next level.
31.why: Each queue snapshot is exactly one level.
31.invariant: head advances one level per outer pass.

31.happening: The queue is empty — every level has been swept.

32.happening: Count the nodes in this level (all currently queued).
32.why: We must process exactly this level before the next fills the queue.
32.invariant: level_size freezes the current level's width.

33.happening: Start an empty row for this level's values.
33.why: Each level becomes one result row.
33.invariant: level collects this depth's values left to right.

34.happening: Start counting nodes processed in this level.
34.why: We stop after level_size of them.
34.invariant: j counts within the current level.

35.happening: More nodes remain in this level — visit the next.
35.why: We process the whole level before moving deeper.
35.invariant: Exactly level_size nodes are visited here.

35.why: All of its nodes have been visited.
35.invariant: The queue now holds only the next level.

36.happening: Look at the node at the front of the queue.
36.why: BFS visits the earliest-queued node next.

37.why: This node is being visited now.

38.happening: Remember this node as the one being visited.
38.invariant: cur_id points at the active node.

39.why: It has been reached and its value recorded.
39.invariant: Visited nodes are never processed again.

40.happening: Fetch the node object from the lookup.
40.why: We need its value and children.
40.invariant: node is the object for node_id.

41.happening: Append the node's value to this level's row.
41.why: Level order emits values left to right within a level.

42.happening: Does this node have a left child?
42.why: Children join the queue for the next level.

43.happening: Enqueue the left child for the next level.
43.why: It will be visited one level deeper.
43.invariant: Children are queued in left-to-right order.

44.invariant: Each node enters the queue once.

45.happening: Does this node have a right child?
45.why: The right child joins the queue after the left.

46.happening: Enqueue the right child for the next level.
46.why: It will be visited one level deeper.
46.invariant: Right follows left in the queue.

47.invariant: Each node enters the queue once.

48.happening: Move to the next node in this level.
48.why: We keep going until the level is done.

49.happening: The level is complete — add its row to the result.
49.why: One finished level becomes one output row.

50.happening: Every level is done — return the traversal: {result}.
50.why: The queue is empty, so the tree is fully swept.

init.happening: Building the tree from the array and seeding the queue with the root.
init.why: We need node objects and links before we can sweep the tree.
init.invariant: Nodes pop in and connect to their parents.

check.happening: Deciding whether a slot is a real node or a child exists.
check.why: null slots are skipped; only real children are enqueued.
check.invariant: Every real node is handled exactly once.

loop.happening: Sweeping the tree one level at a time.
loop.why: Level-order visits all nodes at a depth before going deeper.
loop.invariant: Each outer pass processes one full level.

move.happening: Advancing the sweep — dequeuing the front node.
move.why: BFS visits nodes in the order they were queued.

update.happening: Recording a value and queuing children for the next level.
update.why: State must reflect visited nodes and the growing result.
update.invariant: Visited nodes stay visited; children queue in order.

return.happening: Sweep complete — returning the level-order traversal.
